title: Chettinad Nandu Masala (Crab Curry)

ingredients.name: mud crab (nandu), cleaned and cut into pieces

subs.name: 600 g tiger prawns, peeled — reduce simmer to 6 minutes
60 ml thick coconut milk stirred in at the final step

steps.text: Dry roast the fennel seeds and black pepper in the kadai for 1 minute until fragrant. Transfer to a mixie with the grated coconut and 3 tablespoons of water, and grind to a smooth paste. Set aside.
Heat gingelly oil in the kadai on medium. Splutter mustard seeds, then add star anise and curry leaves. Add shallots and fry until golden, about 5 minutes.
Add ginger-garlic paste and fry 2 minutes. Add tomatoes with a pinch of salt and cook, pressing, until they collapse completely and oil separates — about 5 minutes.
Lower the heat. Add red chilli powder, coriander powder and turmeric. Stir for 30 seconds to bloom the spices without burning them.

Add the crab pieces and toss to coat in the masala. Raise the flame to high and fry for 3 minutes, stirring. Pour in 200 ml hot water, season with salt, cover and cook on medium for 12 minutes until the crab shells turn deep orange-red.
Stir in the coconut-pepper paste. Cook uncovered on medium-high for 5 minutes, stirring frequently, until the gravy thickens and coats the crab. Taste and adjust salt.
Scatter coriander leaves over the curry and serve immediately with rice or parotta. Provide finger bowls — eating nandu masala with fingers is half the joy.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Cook seafood until safely done for the species and preparation; keep it chilled before cooking.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: crab → 600 g tiger prawns, peeled — reduce simmer to 6 minutes; gingelly-oil → coconut oil; coconut → 60 ml thick coconut milk stirred in at the final step; shallot → 1 large onion, sliced
